semantic.role: Defines pattern-based rules for parsing and indexing import, export, and usage structures in Scala source code within the ArchSpine project.
responsibilities: Defines export patterns for functions, classes, case classes, objects, traits, sealed traits, val/var declarations, and type aliases.
Provides usage patterns for function calls (`$NAME($$$ARGS)`) and property accesses (`$OBJ.$NAME`).
outOfScope: Does not cover other languages beyond Scala (though patterns are Scala-specific).
Does not include patterns for control flow, error handling, or macro expansions.
No rules for module-level or package-level scanning logic.

English.purpose: This document is the language pattern configuration for ArchSpine, enabling it to parse and index Scala source files by identifying key code structures (imports, exports, and usages). It bridges the gap between raw source code and ArchSpine’s internal dependency graph.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers maintaining the ArchSpine mirror system—specifically those responsible for language support, code extraction, or dependency analysis. The configuration is Scala-centric but serves as a template for adding other languages.
key_takeaways: Patterns are regex-like but use placeholders like `$NAME`, `$SOURCE`, `$ARGS` for flexible matching.
Exports are categorized by semantic kind (Function, Class, Interface, Variable, Type) to build a structured code model.
The document is part of ArchSpine’s modular language parser; changing it directly affects how Scala code is understood by the system.
Imports use a simple pattern, while exports cover a broad range of Scala declarations, indicating thorough language support.
